commit 818b5c9e398896fe42b52c74a09cd71c2ffa4414
Author: Test User <test@ddx.test>
Date:   Wed Apr 22 21:43:53 2026 -0400

    fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id [ddx-05b4cc9d]
    
    GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID) compared the GraphQL project id argument
    directly to WorkerRecord.ProjectRoot (a filesystem path), so every worker
    was filtered out whenever a non-empty id was passed. The per-project
    Workers view on the project home page was therefore always empty even
    when a Drain-queue dispatch had started a worker.
    
    Resolve the id to its canonical project path via ServerState.GetProjectByID
    before filtering, matching the approach already used by GetBeadSnapshots.
    An unknown project id returns an empty list (not an error), consistent
    with the prior behavior for non-matching filters.
    
    Chose Option 1 (id->path resolution at the filter site) over Option 2
    (store project id on WorkerRecord): one-line change, no schema or
    migration, no coupling between WorkerManager and ServerState required.
    
    Tests:
    - TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new): two registered projects
      with distinct paths/ids, one worker each; asserts workersByProject
      returns only the matching worker per project id, unknown id returns
      empty, and the global workers query still returns both.
    - TestGraphQLWorkers (updated): now queries by the registered project
      id instead of the working-directory path, so the assertion actually
      exercises the resolver rather than relying on the bug.
    
    Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
    acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly.

 cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go         |  19 +++-
 2 files changed, 153 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

    An unknown project id returns an empty list (not an error), consistent

    Chose Option 1 (id->path resolution at the filter site) over Option 2
    (store project id on WorkerRecord): one-line change, no schema or

    Tests:
    - TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new): two registered projects

diff --git a/cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go b/cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
index 86faba69..6f3f6cb2 100644
--- a/cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
+++ b/cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
@@ -256,11 +256,26 @@ func projectEntryToGQL(e ProjectEntry) *ddxgraphql.Project {
 
 // GetWorkersGraphQL implements ddxgraphql.StateProvider.
 // Reads worker records from disk and returns them as GraphQL Worker values.
-// If projectID is non-empty, only workers for that project root are returned.
+// If projectID is non-empty, only workers for that project are returned.
+// An unknown projectID returns an empty list (not an error).
+//
+// WorkerRecord stores the project's filesystem path in ProjectRoot, but the
+// GraphQL argument is a project id (e.g. proj-96d7ea83). Resolve the id to a
+// path via the project registry before filtering so the two representations
+// match; previously this compared id→path directly and filtered out every
+// worker on per-project queries.
 func (s *ServerState) GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID string) []*ddxgraphql.Worker {
 	if s.workingDir == "" {
 		return nil
 	}
+	expectedPath := ""
+	if projectID != "" {
+		proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)
+		if !ok {
+			return nil
+		}
+		expectedPath = proj.Path
+	}
 	workersDir := filepath.Join(s.workingDir, ".ddx", "workers")
 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(workersDir)
 	if err != nil {
@@ -279,7 +294,7 @@ func (s *ServerState) GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID string) []*ddxgraphql.Worker {
 		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &rec); err != nil {
 			continue
 		}
-		if projectID != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {
+		if expectedPath != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {
 			continue
 		}
 		out = append(out, workerFromRecord(rec))

diff --git a/cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go b/cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go
index fe847e59..21315804 100644
--- a/cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go
+++ b/cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go
@@ -166,8 +166,12 @@ func TestGraphQLWorkers(t *testing.T) {
 		t.Error("expected non-empty cursor on worker edge")
 	}
 
-	// Query.workersByProject — scoped to the working dir.
-	body2 := gqlPost(t, srv, fmt.Sprintf(`{ workersByProject(projectID: %q) { totalCount } }`, workDir))
+	// Query.workersByProject — scoped by the registered project id (not path).
+	proj, ok := srv.state.GetProjectByPath(workDir)
+	if !ok {
+		t.Fatalf("expected workDir %q to be registered as a project", workDir)
+	}
+	body2 := gqlPost(t, srv, fmt.Sprintf(`{ workersByProject(projectID: %q) { totalCount } }`, proj.ID))
 	if errs := gqlErrors(body2); len(errs) > 0 {
 		t.Fatalf("workersByProject GraphQL errors: %v", errs)
 	}
@@ -211,6 +215,136 @@ func TestGraphQLWorkers(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 }
 
+// TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID verifies that workersByProject filters
+// by the project id (not path). Regression test for ddx-05b4cc9d: the resolver
+// used to compare the id argument to WorkerRecord.ProjectRoot (a path), so the
+// per-project workers view was always empty for any non-empty project id.
+func TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID(t *testing.T) {
+	xdgDir := t.TempDir()
+	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDir)
+	t.Setenv("DDX_NODE_NAME", "gql-workers-scope-test-node")
+
+	// Project A is the server's own working dir (auto-registered by New()).
+	// Project B is a second registered project with a different path / id.
+	workDirA := setupTestDir(t)
+	workDirB := t.TempDir()
+
+	startedAt := time.Date(2026, 4, 20, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
+	writeWorkerRecord(t, workDirA, WorkerRecord{
+		ID:          "wk-scope-A",
+		Kind:        "execute-loop",
+		State:       "running",
+		ProjectRoot: workDirA,
+		Harness:     "claude",
+		StartedAt:   startedAt,
+	})
+	// Worker B lives under the workers dir of the server's working directory
+	// (that is where GetWorkersGraphQL reads from) but targets project B via
+	// its ProjectRoot field — mirroring how ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.ProjectRoot
+	// redirects a worker to another registered project.
+	writeWorkerRecord(t, workDirA, WorkerRecord{
+		ID:          "wk-scope-B",
+		Kind:        "execute-loop",
+		State:       "running",
+		ProjectRoot: workDirB,
+		Harness:     "claude",
+		StartedAt:   startedAt.Add(time.Minute),
+	})
+
+	srv := New(":0", workDirA)
+	projA, ok := srv.state.GetProjectByPath(workDirA)
+	if !ok {
+		t.Fatalf("project A at %q not registered", workDirA)
+	}
+	projB := srv.state.RegisterProject(workDirB)
+
+	if projA.ID == projB.ID {
+		t.Fatalf("expected distinct project ids, got %q", projA.ID)
+	}
+
+	queryTotal := func(q string) int {
+		t.Helper()
+		body := gqlPost(t, srv, q)
+		if errs := gqlErrors(body); len(errs) > 0 {
+			t.Fatalf("GraphQL errors for %q: %v", q, errs)
+		}
+		var resp struct {
+			Data struct {
+				Workers struct {
+					TotalCount int `json:"totalCount"`
+					Edges      []struct {
+						Node struct {
+							ID string `json:"id"`
+						} `json:"node"`
+					} `json:"edges"`
+				} `json:"workersByProject,omitempty"`
+				Global struct {
+					TotalCount int `json:"totalCount"`
+					Edges      []struct {
+						Node struct {
+							ID string `json:"id"`
+						} `json:"node"`
+					} `json:"edges"`
+				} `json:"workers,omitempty"`
+			} `json:"data"`
+		}
+		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSON for %q: %v", q, err)
+		}
+		return resp.Data.Workers.TotalCount + resp.Data.Global.TotalCount
+	}
+
+	// workersByProject(projectID: projA.ID) must return exactly worker A.
+	workersForProject := func(id string) []string {
+		t.Helper()
+		q := fmt.Sprintf(`{ workersByProject(projectID: %q) { totalCount edges { node { id } } } }`, id)
+		body := gqlPost(t, srv, q)
+		if errs := gqlErrors(body); len(errs) > 0 {
+			t.Fatalf("GraphQL errors for %q: %v", q, errs)
+		}
+		var resp struct {
+			Data struct {
+				WorkersByProject struct {
+					TotalCount int `json:"totalCount"`
+					Edges      []struct {
+						Node struct {
+							ID string `json:"id"`

data.text:
All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by prior attempts merged to main and present in HEAD:

- **AC1 fix** — `state_graphql.go:271-297` (commit `818b5c9e`): resolves `projectID` to project path via `GetProjectByID` before filtering.
- **AC2 regression test** — `TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID` in `graphql_backfill_test.go` — passes.
- **AC3** — N/A since Option 1 was chosen (no `ProjectID` field added to `WorkerRecord`).
- **AC4 Playwright test** — `actions.spec.ts:129-226` covers Drain → Workers tab, plus cross-project isolation (commit `5f234aad`).
- **AC5** — `TestGraphQLWorkers` also passes (global `workers` query still works); worker detail and progress resolvers untouched.
- **AC6** — Cross-reference to `ddx-69789664` present in commit `818b5c9e` message.

The review findings in the bead `<notes>` are stale — they reflect an earlier diff before attempts 1 and 2 landed. This third attempt has nothing to add. Writing a no-changes rationale rather than a redundant commit.

input.content:
Bead ddx-05b4cc9d: no new changes needed — all AC already satisfied by prior
execute-bead attempts merged to main and present at the base rev of this
worktree (a21580e1).

## What is done

AC1 — Root-cause fix (Option 1, id→path at filter site)
    cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go lines 267-299, landed in commit
    818b5c9e "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". GetWorkersGraphQL now calls s.GetProjectByID(projectID),
    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

AC2 — Two-project regression test
    cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go —
    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both
      * workersByProject(id=unknown) returns empty (no GraphQL error)
    TestGraphQLWorkers was also updated to query by resolved project id,
    so it actually exercises the fix.

    Verified locally:
      $ cd cli && go test -run "TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID|TestGraphQLWorkers" ./internal/server/ -v
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkers (0.01s)
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (0.01s)
      ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server

AC3 — Backward-compat test for pre-migration worker records
    N/A. AC3 is conditional on choosing Option 2 (add ProjectID field to
    WorkerRecord). Option 1 was chosen; WorkerRecord schema is unchanged
    and no migration path exists, so the compatibility concern does not
    apply.

AC4 — Playwright e2e validation
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts lines 129-226, landed
    in commit 5f234aad "test(ui): cover project-scoped drain worker flow
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". The test navigates to the project home page, clicks
    Drain queue, confirms the dialog, waits, navigates to the Workers tab,
    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

Both commits are ancestors of HEAD (a21580e1):
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 818b5c9e HEAD  # true
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 5f234aad HEAD  # true

The files in the worktree already contain the fix and the tests. Running
the tests shows they pass. Re-committing the same changes would produce
an empty diff or duplicate history. A well-justified no-changes is the
correct outcome here; the bead should be closed by the reviewer on the
basis of the evidence already in main.

## What a reviewer should check to confirm

  git log --oneline -- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go \
      cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go \
      cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts
  # expect to see 818b5c9e and 5f234aad

  grep -n expectedPath cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
  # expect the id→path resolution in GetWorkersGraphQL

  cd cli && go test -run TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID ./internal/server/ -v
  # expect PASS
